text:
Purchased a phone through customer support with a promotion of $700 bill credits. Customer support assured me while purchasing the device and told me that I am not eligible. They cheated me and the poor knowledge team responded with false statements and promises.At the end they are not giving me the credits.

Team of experts they say but they are team of manipulators

Worst ever. Bought buy one get one but never got the second phone. Plus I have NEVER EVER gotten more than 2 bars on my iPhone 8+.
They do nothing for long time subscribers! If I could give them a zero I would!

Beware of this company I switched to tmobile and got insurance on 1 of my phones then when I needed to use the insurance they tell me that my phone isn't covered when I clearly have it charged on my bill in front of me and have never removed it because my husband is notorious for breaking phones working in construction so now I have an 1100 dollar broken phone that "isn't covered" that I still have to pay off!! Scam company!!!!

I’m telling you T-Mobile lie a lot. I have been getting excessively charged for no reason and when I called T-Mobile. They had to put a hold so my phone won’t be cut off. The representative assured me that my phone won’t be cut off but after all of the conversation guess what happened? My phone got cut off and when I called they all denied I spoke to somebody. Avoid T-Mobile they bunch of scams!!
Sam with ext 3059596 is the whole mastermind behind the lies. Liers!!!
I been with them for 3years but sorry to say they messed up T-Mobile!!! And screw the whole 3 years And half that I’m been with u guys; u guys lack customer service big time.  that’s how I feel about they shitting service. Crooks!!!

All I wanted was to ask a question and receive an answer: to help with assisting a friend. I called your T-Mobile support listed number (whereby, there wasn't an option to speak directly with anyone). 

While still on hold with your support listed number that stated my wait time would be ninety minutes, I went on your website. Your www.tmobilecom option was an even bigger joke as you only allow for connections to your sales dept, unbeknownst to me. LaShawn was great at letting me know that I'd reached your sales department and not your customer service section.

Suggestion: If you're going to utilize bots to answer chat messages, or to offer options to humans, then have several options available for "potential" customers to choose from and to make their selection. No bot or robot can ever take the place of good ole' customer service: engaging with another human being. I wish your company well! Hopefully, you'll review and consider my suggestion to significantly improve your current customer service status.

I wish I had read these reviews before I went with this company. NEVER AGAIN! Don't say you were not warned after reading this and get screwed like I did and learn the hard way. I was with t-mobile for about 6 months. Last month I paid my prepaid account $43 plus I had $9 credit on my account. A few days later I had to cancel my service due to an emergency and I was told that I will get a full refund for $52 on my credit card in about 5 days.Did not happen, so I called again and was told by another "specialist" that he refunded the $52 manually and I should see the credit in about 4-5 days. Did not happen. So I called again. This time I was told by another "specialist" that my request was denied because I should have asked for the refund before I canceled my service......WHAT?  at that point I realized these crooks are trying to steal my money, Plain and simple. I lost $52, but from reading all these negative reviews it will cost them a lot more than that, and that will put a smile on my face!

I am a Customer with T mobile close to over 18 years .I gave T mobile one star because of Alex out of Virginia he is extremely rude and disrespectful he does not know how to speak with customers .You should not be working in customer service .This is on the internet for every one to see .

I Came to t mobile hoping to do the trade in deal with the iPhone 7 in which I was eligible. They did not take my phone due to a very small crack in the corner of the screen . About 2 weeks later my mom found a trade in deal for sprint and at that time my screen had been cracked  worse than before with half the screen cracked but, sprint still said it was eligible and I went from a cracked iPhone 7 to a brand new mint green iPhone 11

Terrible customer service. We changed service providers and were told our plan would be cancelled. We had a family plan. They did not cancel our plan but kept charging us automatic withdrawl for months afterward claiming 1 line of our "family plan" had not been cancelled. If it was a whole family plan and the plan was cancelled how are they still charging our family? Stay far away from t mobile.  And they refused to reimburse us for the months we paid for service when we weren't getting any service with them

TMobile overbilled on our final bill by charging to our provided credit card, then held the funds for 3 months so they could earn interest on it, then instead of reimbursing the credit card they charged, sent a debit card which took 30 minutes to activate and was a pain to use.  We were only with them for 7 months as their coverage was sub-par.  WARNING...Find another phone service...don't put yourself through their tricks and scams. Mark C.

I called to talk about my last bill. The company can take your payment but They can’t provide you with details of the Final bill. That is a horrible way to do business. They tell you to go to the store.  They should be able to see any and every Bill they collected a payment. It’s a good way to get scammed!

I changed carriers so that my significant other and I would be on the same bill. I have an iPhone 8, I called to pay it off so I would be able to take my phone to my new carrier and not have to buy a new phone. My phone works well and I had just bought a phone case with my dogs face on it so I was not giving it up. Well I couldn’t use my phone because T-Mobile wouldn’t unlock it. I had to go to the store,  when I told the rep the situation his demeanor changed. He went from being excited to help me to not my problem attitude. He told me he couldn’t help me and that I would have to call customer service. At this point I’m locked out from my phone. The screen says I had to contact T-Mobile to unlock my phone. I told him I didn’t have a phone and asked if he could call them, which he did with an attitude. He then said it would take 72 hours for my phone to be unlocked.  I waiting the 72 hours and my phone was still locked so I called T-Mobile. They proceeded to tell me that it could take up to 14 days because they were waiting for Apple to send them paperwork proving that’s I bought the phone and it wasn’t stolen. I was very confused because I bought the phone from T-Mobile not Apple. I asked the woman if I called apple if that would help and she said “ they won’t help you”.  So I called Apple, and they said that was a lie, they don’t send anyone paperwork for anything. This is the way the Apple customer service agent explained it, 
“ we sell lawn motors and we don’t care who’s lawn your mowing.” Absolutely no one at T-Mobile did anything to help me out with my issue. I had to wait, not the 14 days but I had to wait. After being with them for so long and purchasing so much product.

We needed to get my Iphone 8 64gb fixed because it had a crack to the side but it still functions like how it regularly does so we went to the Chandler Fashion Mall T-Mobile store. When we went to the the T- Mobile at the Chandler Fashion Mall, we showed the guy the phone and he quoted "it was damaged beyond repair". Obviously it was still functioning and was not "damaged beyond repair". He did not even attempt to look at it properly and wave it off like it was a lost cause. We naturally asked what are options are and the guy who we talked to was like we could not get it fixed at all even though we have been paying insurance each month. After that, he sent us to the Apple store and the apple store indeed send it to that one T-Mobile store. The Apple store then send us back to the T-Mobile store and we asked for the manager politely if they have the apple phone that was sent to that store. They did have the phone the whole time and did not want to give the phone to us. Despite us being polite to the manager, he is down right disrespectful and still would not relinquish the phone to us. We then went back to the apple store to tell them about the issue and now they are sending the phone to our house but with a fee of $99 when it was not that much damaged that much. The customer service was also terrible because they lead us to goose chase. They basically were testing our patience and we were not going to put up with it. We do not know if they know it was that one manager or what, but we do not want to go back if that manager is still there. It was down right unfair for them to do that. We been with them for 3 years and have good credit with them but still receiving bad service. For those who are going to get the T-Mobile service, our advice to if you are going to get you need to be firm with these people because they do not seem to comprehend when a phone needs to get fixed they need to get fixed. If you do not want to deal with them, then i suggest going to another service to save you the trouble of dealing with this service.

buyer beware... I was told if i purchased the SIMS cards I would have 30 days to return... decided not to go to T-MOBLE and never opened SIM cards ($150.00) worth and they charged my credit card spent hours on the phone; only taked to people over seas per manager overseas they are not allowed to give a direct line or email address everytime i called no notes it was like i never called before.  I am still fighting for my money.  Please do not use T-moble it is a scam.. you can get someone in the US for sales only friendly eager to help once you have a problem no luck... not good customer service I will pay double before I would have them as a carrier.
Worst customer service I've ever received.  DO NOT use t-mobile

When deciding to look at or change a service provided, the term BUYER BEWARE - is the one most important thought that should be on your mind when you walk into a T-mobile store.

Their sales people are either not trained in regard to the plans or worse their only concern is the get you hooked and will tell you anything they want in order to lock you in with there company.

The first misleading statement is that there is no contract -- as far as a phone contract is concerned that maybe true -- however- if they offer you a new phone at no cost and all you have to do is trade in your phone -- do not do it -- the company's policy is to give you a low credit number on your phone say your getting a $600 credit and they're charging you a minimum amount of $1.29 or $1.79 per month for 24 months --once you do this they basically have your derriere for 2 years -- this is how they get you and if you are unhappy with the service which most of the reviews below seem to be and you want to cancel the service they will charge you for the full cost of the phone.

Additionally, they tell you about a plan you agree to and then once your locked in they notify you that you are on the wrong plan and you wind up paying more because of the sales person error.

So again I say to anyone looking at T-Mobile Buyer Beware and make sure you cover all your bases because you won't be happy.

T-Mobile is horrible they charge you prices that are outrageous the customer service when you called and talk to them they’re rude they’re nasty they have attitude they talk to you like you’re stupid. But then when you asked to be transferred to a supervisor then they want to try to get rude or they take forever to put a supervisor on the phone it takes a supervisor an additional 30 minutes to even decide to get on the phone and talk to you and they’re rude and disrespectful to  and then they get back on the phone to sit here to try to keep talking and talking and talking I don’t request anybody going to T-Mobile at all they’re rude and disrespectful!

Though the employees are really busting their buttholes with some phenomenal customer service they can never shovel through the pile of other feces their company and the phone manufacturers insist on producing and hiring others to deal with. They're all indeed champions of eugenics one could only hope that they take their own dogma and indoctrination slicing their own throats  immediately. It's time we took the bureaucracy out of our state it is time we took corporations out of our state and it's time to get out the guillotines and The Hangman's noose and have our way with those maniacal eugenics candidates yeah I don't like T-Mobile I think they should be dismantled with the rest. Just too bad a vast majority of the people are so manipulated and indoctrinated that they can't even see we don't require any of them or they're ridiculous
 megalomaniac companies

I would like to acknowledge the professionalism, respectability, willingness and kindness of Hertlyn S. and Carmen M. at Rt. 422 & Rt. 222, Store 9069, Wyomissing, Pennsylvania...towards me at their T Mobile store when I was experiencing a truly miserable situation with my phone/connection etc recently. I am grateful for such a wonderful team of professionals.
Me and my husband decided to change our phones and walk in to a very happy group of representatives they help us with two new phones help us set them up ! Thank you for such a awesome team !

Had my service disrupted for a month, and was still charged $10 (they only credited for 3 weeks)

Was then charged over $100 for an international phone call, despite having an unlimited plan. Was notified that this charge occurred, but when I went to verify the charge it didn't display anything. When my next bill arrived, it was for $187. 

After calling to request a refund, they offered me a $25 credit on this $100+ charge, despite this being a direct result of their messaging not syncing across push notifications + their website in a timely manner.

Customer service then blamed me for their shady practices, saying it wasn't a T-Mobile error, despite 2 months of nothing BUT T-Mobile errors. Offering someone $25 for charges that should not have been incurred in the first place is a hilariously out-of-touch practice.

The worst cell phone provider I have used in a decade. I have never experienced more issues with inconsistent billing, false charges, and again, totally unempathetic customer service.

This is the worst service I ever had the calls get dropped the internet doesn't work half of the time their phones you pay a higher price for and our pieces of junk so I advise you to run do not get this service

If I could put a negative star review rating I would, for one, the service is ABSOLUTELY TERRIBLE, calls dropping, no bars of service in almost every area. Two, I have reported this problem to managers, the store itself, and just about every person I possibly can, and no one knows what’s going on...I also switched from Verizon to t mobile, during the “trade your device in and get the iPhone 11 on us” Complete BS, I’m currently paying on the iPhone 11 now that was suppose to be “on us”. T mobile is terrible, I will never recommend.

The online chat service person could not answer any real questions about switching, cost per month, upgrading phones, and cost up front. Silly thing...  I'm still attracted to one of their plans but someone that is better needs to chat with me.!!!

Absolutely horrible.
Their service is complete crap, their representatives aren’t educated on their own company and policies, and they have charged me 2 times even though I SWITCHED COMPANIES AND I AM NO LONGER WITH THEM! Do not make the same mistake I did. do not get involved with this company, I promise it will be a regret. Seriously, one star is still giving them too much credit. RUN FROM THIS COMPANY.

I can’t give negative stars or I would!!! Do not use these people i closed an account after two years PAID OFF.  Never Late on any bills  And they sent my account to collections for 300!!! I’m beyond furious and cannot stress enough how criminal this place it not  to mention the service is horrid. Please I beg any one that listens they are thief’s DO NOT use them. Please, please do not give these people your money

T-Mobile is horrible.. The cellular service SUCKS!! My phone doesn’t even get good connection in certain parts of my city. On top of that, the hotspot is complete TRASH! Don’t waste your time with them. Metro PCS is 10x better, & even Boost Mobile is better.. The pre-paid companies always seem to work more efficiently!

T-MOBILE is the worst company i ever dealth with as the child actor from return of the swamping 1990 i have many friends who are still in the acting career. I also played Darian Kain on HBOs' Oz. People are gonna listen. And i promise any of my friends who own stock. Imgoing to buy them and sell at 5 ¢ a share. If you are considering T-Mobile by metro i suggest you do not.

Deactivating my T-Mobile account and resolving my final payment has been the most difficult and frustrating experience that I have ever experienced. I deactivated my account and ported my number over to a new carrier on December 23rd, 3 days into the new billing cycle. When I received my last bill, I noticed that they had charged me for the entire bill cycle up to January 19th, not just the 3 days the service was active. I called and spoke to a representative who confirmed that this was a mistake and that It should be a prorated amount for 3 days. She told me to disregard the current final bill and that I would be receiving an updated final bill in about a month. 2 weeks later I receive a mail notice from a collections agency for the inaccurate amount. I call T-Mobile AGAIN and they tell me that the amount is correct because they have a policy that you will be charged for the full billing cycle regardless of canceling and deactivating your service and that I won’t be receiving a revised bill. I have NEVER in my life heard of a company with such a policy and have never received notice from T-Mobile that this is their policy. I asked to speak with a supervisor since I was being told two completely different things and because they had the audacity to tell me to disregard the final bill but then sent it to a collections agency. The manager said we will review the recording and look more into this, we will let you know what we come to in a week. 

This has been a horrible customer service experience, they should honor what the original representative of T-Mobile told me without drawing this out even more. However, given my experience up to this point, I’m not confident that they will. 

I strongly advise against going with T-Mobile, there are far superior providers with better deals. And if you do go with T-Mobile, be prepared to deactivate your account on the last day of the billing cycle unless you want to pay for days of service that was not used or active.
LOL. I was in the family plan. Now the account holder disappeared and I basically lost my number. The worst!

buyer don't look this way go else ware 

WORST
DO YOUR SELF A FAVOR 
AN HEAD ACHE

We cancelled 1/2 T mobile due to poor connection and frequent call drops. We were told that our bill will be prorated for the next billing cycle since the starts at the beginning of the won’t till the end 1/1 to 1/31. Then we received a bill for the whole month of the January billing cycle. When I called again, T mobile told me that we don’t prorate you have to pay the whole billing cycle even you canceled on the 1/2 so for two days of that cycle we got the whole bill. Furthermore, t-mobile said all cellular company don’t pro rate which i think is a lie because i went to verizon before and sprint and when i canceled they prorate my bill. Tmobile cant even send me a receipt of my final payment via email or mail or anything because I don’t have any account with them anymore so they only give me a confirmation number. When I insist for a receipt the representative even mentioned “when you buy online you only get confirmation number” I almost lost it and had to take couple deep breaths and replied yes you get at least an email of your purchase with the confirmation number... will never go back to t-mobile anymore and my friends are same way.

I just purchased the SyncUP Drive device for my car. Since I am a computer technician, I use mobile hot spot often. I was reading up on this device, and I thought this would be a perfect fit for my job. I have unlimited hot spot on my phone, so I can tether this with the device so I don't have to open my phone everytime I need to get on Wifi. I bought the device, and after about an hour of trying to get the device to recognize in the app, it finally started working.
Here is where it gets interesting: I asked the representative that I was talking to if this was data limited or did it tether to my account. She told me that I had 2GB of hot spot. I was shocked! I said, are you serious? How come the plan details was not explained to me when I bought the device? Had I known that this "hot spot" is limited to 2GB, I would have walked out of the store. And of course, guess who cannot return this new unnecessary device, because the rep told me to just use my phone as the hot spot? Without paying a $50 restocking fee, you are stuck with this device. This is not to say that the device is not handy for keeping an eye on your vehicle, but my main purpose was for the mobile wifi capabilities. The representative told me that I should have relayed this to the person who sold me the device. I explained that, no, your site should explain these plans so the person who is deciding whether or not they want to make this investment, has all the information they need.
Anyway, I used to be a loyal T-Mobile customer. This has changed my opinion as it seems deceitful and dishonest, especially since I just bought it and they want to charge me half the price it costs to restock it back to the store.
If you are looking to purchase this device, please, don't forget to ASK the sales rep about the plans that come with the device because apparently, that is not something they should do, unless you are buying a phone, then they have to ask you what plan you want because, oh, that happens to be an integral part of your mobile device.
**This device was bought at the 400 Veterans Blvd. store**

I needed assistance with some information on my project paper and Clark an accounts expert ID 17769277 helped me where he could. It feels good when your carrier is not just listening when you are buying but even when you need help to write a project paper. I have been with T-Mobile since 2006. I am grateful.

The worst service ever. Everywhere that I go is not signal at all. Even in my yard there is no signal. I called them and complained  but they know that their service is waste even their customer service too. Their answer to me was that they aware about their service but what I got was a local cover area, so I asked them if I had a land line with them, because it was supposed to be a móvil, working where I go, and why do they say nationwide? They are a total liars and corrupt.

After turning in my iPhone X to the store and switching from Verizon, I got on the 2019 iPhone 11 plan where you get it free with your trade in. Everything was good the first two months then they said  they never received my iPhone X and now are charging me the full price of the iPhone 11. Dealing with them for weeks and there's nothing they can do. Worst service ever!!

Horrible customer service. I have been trying for months to reset the pin and email account. But, their antiquated system must send a verification code to the phone. Unfortunately, the phone is not accessible at this time and won't be for a few more months. They say that there is nothing else they can do. I will never use T-mobile again.

I have never experienced worse customer service! The screen on my iPhone 8 plus went black. I took it to a T-Mobile location where they told me I had insurance and apple care and that I should go to the apple store to get my screen replaced for $30. I leave and drive 45 min to the closest apple store and wait 2 hours just for them to tell me I do not have apple care. T-Mobile gave me wrong information. I then had to call T-Mobile to put in an insurance claim in where they apologized for telling me I had apple care because I did not. So now they are ordering me a new phone to be shipped to my closest T-Mobile store. The lady on the phone was suppose to call me back in one hour to finalize the order and I gave her an alternative number to reach me at and sure enough she doesn't call that number she calls my broken phone! So now theres no way for me to answer it and I have to call customer service back and wait on hold AGAIN! I finally get in touch with someone they confirm they are sending my phone within 1-2 business days and will be covering the cost to expedite my package due to the inconvenience. So now on the second business day I go to pick up my phone and my package is at the T-Mobile store addressed to MY NAME and the representative in the store is ringing me out and tells me there is something wrong it wont let her ring me out. So she calls customer service to help her and the lady on the phone whose name was ANNAKATRI and her last name initial is B has the girl in the store that was working with me CANCEL the order for my phone that was in my hands at the store! She then has the girl try to put a new order in to see if that will ring me out and it came back as saying the order was pending. This representative on the phone is suppose to be helping the T-Mobile workers in the store with issues and she did nothing but make it worse! She then said she was going to transfer us to a manager for help. The manager said he didn't know why ANNAKATRI had her cancel the first order because now there is nothing they can do and a new phone is now being sent out again to me and I have to wait for that new phone now when my first ordered phone was in my hands in the store! Since ANNAKATRI did not know what she was doing now I have to wait for another phone in the mail! I also cannot work without my phone and cannot access my laptop without my security information on my phone! I am now on my third day of not being able to work because of the horrible service with T-Mobile. I called customer service again after all this to make sure my phone was being next day shipped and that they were paying to do so and the man on the phone told me it is being shipped next day and gave me the customer representative that advised the T-Mobile worker to cancel my first orders name (ANNAKATRI). Hopefully my phone will be here tomorrow morning like they have promised yet again. I am extremely dissatisfied with their service and will begin shopping for another cell carrier. Not one person in this whole process with T-Mobile seemed to know what they were doing and I will be not be a customer for much longer!
Anthony is the absolute best. 40523. call him every single time you wont be disappointed. Solves problems quickly and efficiently and overall a friendly and kind person.

Hello everyone.I switched to T-Mobile from Verizon.I herd some good things about T-Mobile but I was sadly mistaken. T-Mobile has been the worst phone service I have ever had I'm very disappointed in this carrier and will let everyone know how bad it really is I tried to get out of it but they would not let me.

BEWARE!!!!!! Worst customer service. Apparently I was under the impression that switching away from T mobile was going to be easy. 4 months later and I'm STILL getting bills. What a terrible customer service they have. Do not go with them!!!!

T-Mobil does bait and switch with new customer! 
Currently filing a law suit against them, because of this store 1735 Martin Luther King Drive, Bronx, NY.  I went to make an order for the New iphone 11 max pro, but they where not in stock for 2 more months. So the manager talks me into taking the Xr model meanwhile  with premium insurance and his word that he will switch out my 2 phones with the 11 Max Pro with no further deposits when they do arrive.   When I go to switch out he actually says I need to give him another deposit of $400 each for the 11 Max Pro because he meant only the regular 11 is covered and that he never promised me  the Max pro 11. I left Metro Pc for a phone didn’t really want, and I even purchased an I- watch for my wife! Customer  service says hey sorry you got taken but we don’t have anything in writing so tuff luck!
5 stars for Jazmin 23131. Thanks for your patience.

T-Mobile allowed my cellular account to be compromised while I was out of the country. This transpired by allowing a "Sim Swap" at one of their locations. This lead unauthorized users to gain access to my personal cell number and emails. They used this access to reset passwords which provided them access to certain funds and transferred them out of my account.

After numerous calls to T-Mobile who promised that once an investigation was complete (estimated 7 days), a representative from their investigation department would call me back. However, someone only called me back only after I called them the fourth time. Although they were polite and apologetic at all times over the phone, they are not willing to reimburse me with my financial loss as a result of their employees lack of security controls. 

To all out there - beware.......

T-Mobile does not care about their  Customers. I have had a note 9 and it worked well for a while when it  Went badd I returned it for a soul called like new phone which is actually a used phone that doesn't work either this is my 3rd time returning a phone to them because the ones they send me do not work either. They refused to give me a new phone that works I have had this awful experience in the path as well with the Samsung Galaxy A8 which I had to return I believe 4 times before they finally gave me a new phone that work very now my only option is to buy a brand new phone and start my payments over from square 1. And I have a so called warrantee as welcke's as so called insurance that I pay for. You madigan is U has a warranty and insurance on a new automobile that you buy and you get it halfway paid for and something goes wrong so you take it to the dealer under their warranty and they say we can't help you however we can so you a brand new car and you can start making your payments all over again. That pretty much the scam they're running as well as their insurance which I pay $15 a month for Please excuse my rambling I am using the voice text and I'm not very good at it I would advise all new consumers to look elsewhere Thank you

I'm currently traveling, and I decided to get an e-sim to avoid the hassle of switching sim cards. My brother previously called to set up his own e-sim, and he was able to complete the process without visiting a store. In addition, the T-Mobile website clearly states that I can call their customer service line in order to start the e-sim process. Their customer service representative and supervisor started the process, but then told me they were unable to complete it because I would have to physically visit a store. They said it was a "new" process, and that the website was wrong. They offered no solutions either. Terrible service.

Big time scam company, I have for almost 6 months called multiple times and asking them to send me my monthly statement in writing to my email and home adress but it never comes. Agreed monthly plan cost were always different and much higher. I canceled my plan with them in December with a confirmation but they still charged me for January. After multiple attempts by sending email and calling them they still refuse to send out a  cancelation confirmation. 

Stay away from this scammer company. 

Always ask for everything in writing from them before you sign.

1 time I went to a T Mobile store to complain about my speaker it was hard to hear and had to have people to repeat themselves. Was given direction to take it back to a store if problem continues. I did, they ran me thru the hoops with Tech for 30 mins then check the phone out for damage then ordered the new phone. Got the message to pick up the new phone went to T-Mobile to pick it up. They checked the phone and said they could not accept it, there was dust in the speaker and the finger pint sensor was scratched. T Mobile would not issue the phone that was at the store. Left the store after letting the store Associate Bri P. my thoughts on how poor their customer support was, to have a phone at their store that was sent to me and it not being issued was disturbing, upsetting and frustrating. Left a T-Mobile phone store with a broke phone. Called customer care T-mobile who connected me with insurance saying 0 deductible. Spoke with Trent, ok your deductable is $ 99. But I was told it was 0. No but you can call and talk to them . I did and now I can go to Best Buy 45miles away and have them look at it or repair it if they can. It might be cheaper than the $99 insurance would charge. Thanks for putting the Customer not 1st but without and last place.

This company is ABSOLUTELY RIDICULOUS! My husband and I are paying for insurance on both of our phones. His screen is cracked so bad its dangerous to use. Yes he used it for a week or 2 until we had the money. Then, he called to file a claim and pay them some more money on top of the almost 300 we already pay, and they denied his claim because he had switched to his very old t mobile phone so he could function and he couldn't remember the exact date he used it last! I ordered an iPhone XR on the phone via customer care and a couple of hours later I changed my mind and called Customer Care to cancel order and instead told her I want to place a new order for an iPhone 11. She then told me they can not cancel it as it’s already been processed for shipping. I was told they are going to send me a return label via email so I can put it over on the original package label then take it to UPS drop off location. She also said I will not be able to get a refund of the tax I paid for it till they get the iPhone XR and get it scanned into the system So I said okay fine... 
Few days later the IPhone XR got delivered by UPS .. I never opened the box as I am returning it and I don’t want the XR anyway. Only time I held the box when attaching new return package label sent by T-Mobile. Moving forward....The following morning the Package was dropped off at a UPS drop off location in Houston.

Same day I placed a new order for an iPhone 11 128 gig via Customer Care and paid applicable tax and waited for my package to arrive. Three days later I have a notification from UPS that the iPhone 11 is for delivery at my physical address. Waited all day ...by 8:15 pm that day I called UPS and told them the package never came and that I will pick it up at the UPS location the following day. As I got there the following day....UPS told me that they can not locate the package (IPhone 11 128 gig) but the status still for delivery. At that point I concluded It’s missing so I reported it to T-Mobile- I was on the phone with T-Mobile and UPS supervisor for 2 hours. As a summary of this ordeal it was determined that it got lost by UPS and I have to wait for a week to close the investigation before I will get my refund of the tax I paid for it. It never got delivered- got stolen as simple as that. I did get a refund eventually but took several calls to make it happen.

I ended up getting my IPhone 11 at the T-Mobile store as I don’t trust any more packages in the mail. To this date on the T-Mobile apps still saying that the lost package  IPhone 11 128 gig is for delivery which is ridiculous. 

So back to the IPhone XR. On December 2, 2019 I received a UPS notification that the tracking number for the XR package had been delivered at their Fort Worth Tx Warehouse. So I called customer care to verify if they received it. I was told by many customer care representatives that they don’t have it yet each time I call and that it will take weeks to get it scanned into the system. It’s started to annoy me but I was still being patient about the whole thing although it had gotten to a point where i feel how could it happen to me twice in just weeks apart.... two packages. I was being lied to many times by these people as to what happened to the IPhone XR package.

I got so fed up of their unprofessional treatments with me as they can not give me a straight answer. All I was getting from them - "we will initiate the investigation" each time I call. They don’t even like to talk to me anymore as if they put a star next to my name in their computer system like here comes the ** she’s calling again. A week before Christmas I called and ask for a supervisor with no result at all. The supervisor named Yvatt was not nice and not very helpful at all. Two customer care representatives that I talked to seemed to be a little helpful but there’s nothing they can do to resolve the issue of finding the missing package or giving me back my $49 refund 
 Telling me over and over again, "We will continue to look for it." Bear in mind this a week before Christmas and my patience was about to run out. The answers I get each time I call -- They can not find the package that they will continue to look for it.... we will initiate an investigation at least I was told more than a few times. How could you be starting a new one when there are already in progressed investigations Re: XR package 
And I said go ahead as I want closure of this account... paying for something you don’t have. 

Moving forward by Jan 9, 2020 I called again and I talked to a customer care representative by the named of Bruce who seemed to be helpful at that time. I gave him the tracking information again and he told me he will call me in about a week 1/16/20. He never did. So I called him with all the printouts of my tracking information and proof of delivery and who received it. He was extremely rude and unprofessional I was so shocked when this T-Mobile customer care representative Bruce told me that they found the box it’s empty and that I had returned an empty box. He then demanded that I returned the IPhone XR so they can close up the finance account as they were charging me for a device that I don’t have. Plain and simple T-Mobile accused me of stealing it, that I opened the box and keep the phone. I had the package clear taped around it so no one can tamper it or open it.

It took them a month and a half to tell me this. It's beyond belief. They claimed the box was found empty on December 9, 2019 and it took them over a month to give me this information. The fact that this item has been missing for awhile, T-Mobile did not take a picture of the outside of the box but only the inside once they found the package. Common sense to take a picture of the outside of the box to prove there is no tampering of this package. That is a missing package and you threw away the evidence. That tells you T-Mobile is covering up something, telling me they don’t have the box anymore. I can not prove that it was stolen in the warehouse as there is no picture of the outside box being tempered with. In a polite way, they want me to give the phone back to them which I don’t have. They even put a restriction on me to not starting an investigation with UPS as to what happen to the phone. That tells me something is not right. UPS told me T-Mobile restricted it. The fact that if a device is missing they should have blocked the IMEI so nobody can activate it. They were able to blocked IMEI of the missing iPhone 11 128 gig that UPS lost but not the XR? And why being restricted me to open an investigation with UPS? 

If you order an iPhone get it at the store so you won’t end up in the same predicament like I did. To this date there is no resolution. The fact is I sent the phone. I have proof of delivery but doesn’t mean anything at all. All they are saying I have the missing phone and I sent an empty box. T-Mobile doesn’t care about customers satisfaction unlike before when they have loyalty department.

T-Mobile relies their Texas Customers Service people who are very unprofessional. Supervisors are horrible. I can only remember a couple of the customer care representatives who are in a way helpful in the beginning however the rest I gave them a thumbs down. After a total of 15 years with T-Mobile it's time to move on. It’s not worth of my time. I can not deal any more stress with this company. A horrible experienced with them. I would not recommend T-Mobile for a phone provider especially if you lived in Houston, Texas. Customer Care is horrible. Outside Texas maybe okay. Also buy your phone at the store if you are happy with T-Mobile. However do not let them send to you by mail especially via UPS and avoid returning an iPhone to a T-Mobile warehouse Fort Worth if you don’t want to be in the same predicament like I did.

Hmm, some of the experiences I have read here are what I experienced with Verizon! Verizon is the reason I switched to T-Mobile! I was with Verizon for 22 years with a perfect account (no lates or anything) and since 2017 it has been an absolute nightmare. Customer service is literally impossible to reach!

I switched to T-Mobile about one month ago and the experience from day one has been the exact opposite of Verizon. I started calling T-Mobile a month before I switched peppering them with questions and concerns and being very skeptical. I heard promises that I also heard from Verizon which made me hesitate, but there has been one difference: T-Mobile has made good on every one of them! Customer service is dead easy to reach and every rep before sale and after sale has been personable and professional with the juice to get things done!
I just got off the phone with them again telling them of my experiences with them and strongly stating that I hope this is not just a honeymoon! So far, everything they have promised and done has been backed up in writing as well!
As for coverage, I was reluctant to leave Verizon coverage, but so far T-Mobile has proven to be at least a close second if not equal! On top of that, my monthly bill is $8 less than Verizon and I have unlimited minutes. With Verizon I had 700 minutes (although I never came close to using even 100 minutes in a month).
So far, T-mobile has been a pleasure.

UPDATE:Well, I need to lower my star rating by at least one. I have now been with T-Mobile for 1.5 months and the writing is on the wall now. I’m afraid it may be the beginning of the end for T-Mobile. I have recently started receiving ads via phone and email for “T-Mobile medical alert alarms” and “T-Mobile Banking Services”.
Neither of these have anything to do with mobile phone service and represent a desperate money grab attempt. Too many pots on the burners and a few will boil over. I have seen this happen over a hundred times with other companies, both similar and different. They start offering unrelated services and get overwhelmed with negative experiences because they lose focus on the core business. Eventually, customer service suffers to the point of disappearing all together and employee moral goes in the toilet as all the service reps and technicians are fired. Customers can’t get help and eventually the company goes under. Sometimes it takes a few years and sometimes a few months. 
I left Verizon after 22 years due to this exact thing. They no longer have any customer service and tell you flat out that they don’t care. They don’t even have a customer retention team anymore!
Next, I expect to see T-Mobile offering car insurance, life insurance and other scams. 
If T-Mobile keeps going down this path, they will fold. I’ll be leaving them before that happens.

Poor customer service. With the store located near the Florida Mall in Orlando on sand lake road. I am a handicapped person using mobile for 18 years loyal customer.  I wanted help with my new phone from t mobile 
I was kept waiting for most 3 hrs with no help from anyone of the people working there which I believe were from another country. I will not mention names. As a handicapped person I needed to use the bathroom and the response was we don't have one "go next door to use the bathroom"
They were not able to help me with the phone that t mobile had sent to me a few days ago. Finaally my uberdriver who had nothing to do with t mobile was helping me while the people working in the state were staring helplessly at me. Not even a word of apology while they were gossiping about their social life among themselves. What a shame. Never sign up with t mobile.

Been researching for weeks on switching from Verizon to t mobile. 

Encountered door to door sales people at the business. Introduced me to sensorly.com assured me great reception + LTE coverage. Spoke with several t mobile employees at the stores who also assured great cellular coverage and LTE coverage. 

I began getting dead zones and choppy LTC. I spoke with t mobile representative they thought it was because my iPhone 7 was didn’t have a strong antenna- made sense. New phone, new technology so I would get better reception with a newer phone. However, when I got my iPhone 11 pro max it was the same.  Dead zones and choppy LTC. 

The apps on my phone that rely on LTC are: Pandora, google maps, Waze, yelp, and Gmail. Without LTC these apps don’t work. I use these apps daily and most of the time when I’m outside of the house where I don’t have WiFi. Not only is it embarrassing have my Waze stall on
Me but it’s very inconvenient. 

I recently had to go to the airport to pick up and take out relatives. My Waze and pandora wouldn’t work. There were times when we would sit and wait to get a LTC signal. I ended up driving around to get connected to LTC so that I could resume their vacation. 

Everyday I get off work. I call my wife to catch up with her and her day. With a newborn at home, I enjoy my wife telling me about his development. Right when the conversation starts to get good. The call drops. We’ve finally pinpoint the exact location where my calls get dropped. There is a block of street where I ALWAYS get my call dropped. Whenever I get near it now, I tell my wife I’m entering the dead zone- it is now a daily routine. 

On my days off I enjoy going out and enjoying the day. I enjoy yelping and eating. When we’re out for lunch. Sometimes we would yelp our next location for dessert. There would be times when I dint have LTC so yelp doesn’t work. 

It may be a minor inconvenience here and there. But it NEVER happened to me while I was with Verizon. Cellular and LTC with t mobile is not great. Im Not happy with my 2 year contract.

For the past year the professionalism of the customer care call in people and the tech support people has gone down considerably. My service was good for the 3 first year and now I'm going to switch to Verizon

We ordered a new cell phone with T-mobile Rep over the phone on 12/18/19 late night and he took our credit card, said he would send us document to sign, then charge our card. Next morning, we still didn't get the email, but the credit card was charged for the full price. So we called T-mobile and found out they would not send us anything to sign, the order had not processed but our card was charged and the phone would not be shipped out in 2 days. We worried it would not get here in time for Christmas, so we asked her to cancel it. The Rep took the cancellation. Then she called back, said even they had not processed it yet, but she could not cancel the order, to get the refund, we need to refuse the shipment when it gets to us. We spoke to a supervisor, she insisted on their system would not let her cancel it, even everything happened within 12 hours and shipment has not even processed. So we had to wait for UPS shipment, then refused the package. But two weeks after the package refusal, we still have not seen our refund. We called T-mobile again, then they said they received the returned package, but they have not processed the refund yet, told us wait for another week. Now it has been almost 4 weeks, we still have not seen our refund for a phone order we wanted to cancel within 12 hours of the ordering.  We just called again, the auto message said the waiting time is 45 mins. for someone to call us back. Overall, this was a very disappointing experience! We have been T-mobile customers for a long time, but we will not buy any new phones from T-mobile in the future anymore. We know eventually, we can get our money back from our credit card for a phone we have never seen, but the way they run customers around and waste their time is just not acceptable.

If I could give t mobiles customer service (Call Center)a 0 I would...I absolutely love going into the t mobile store, they are always courteous and friendly, they go above and beyond to make sure the customer is satisfied. My issue is with the call center associates...They are rude and unprofessional...They do not do what they say they are going to do, bad business practices...I just recently started services with them and I wanted to add another line. The store associate informed me that I would have to pay a significant amount down because I didn’t have a lot of credit left. She called customer service and they sent her to consumer credit. The lady in consumer credit said she increased my credit limit so that I could add the line, she said it would change within an hour. So I left the store and came back only to find that nothing had changed. The only thing she did was put a note in my account that the credit limit was increased, she never actually increased it. At this point the store manager got involved and she called consumer credit back and asked why would they tell us that they increased our limit but didn’t make the change, she advised him that the previous rep notated what the intentions were. He told her that it couldn’t be increased, he was sooooo rude. We spoke to several different managers who claimed they could not honor what their representative said, even though it was notated...Verizon  may be expensive but they do exactly what they promise, they are all about making the customers happy, and they honor what they say they’re going to do...i regret switching over to T-Mobile...I wish t mobiles store representatives could do more because the call center representatives do not care about the customers!!!
